content.theorem: WASM + TypeScript bridge enables near-native numerical performance in the browser with type-safe JavaScript wrappers
content.proof: Compile Rust/C snap kernels to WASM (2-4KB). Load via ES modules in TypeScript. Type-safe wrapper functions handle the JS/WASM boundary. For visualization: WebGL canvas renders 100K+ lattice points at 60fps. The browser becomes a viable platform for interactive mathematical exploration.
content.benchmark: WASM snap in browser: ~800M ops/sec (vs native 2.27B). WebGL lattice render: 60fps at 100K points. Canvas 2D: 30fps at 10K points.
content.caveat: WASM has no SIMD in all browsers yet. WASM GC proposal is still experimental. The JS/WASM boundary has ~10ns overhead per call.
